sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Tesla Short Sellers Gain $9 Billion as Shares Tumble
feed.summary: Tesla Inc. stock recently dropped significantly after reporting weaker-than-expected second-quarter earnings, leading to substantial gains for short sellers. Short sellers pocketed $4.3 billion in mark-to-market gains from a single day's selloff, and their year-to-date unrealized gains reached $9.08 billion for 2026, making Tesla the most shorted stock among the Magnificent Seven. The company's high valuation and investments in future technologies like AI and robotics are raising questions about its growth prospects.

feed.title: SFGV ETF Profile: Dividends, Returns (BOATS:SFGV)
feed.summary: The SFGV ETF invests globally in equity securities, allocating 50-75% to US, 20-30% to other developed, and the rest to emerging markets, with allocations adjusting based on risk/return. It screens for positive shareholder yield and free cash flow yield, targeting attractively valued companies and strong-performing ETFs. The fund, issued by Empirical Finance LLC, has an expense ratio of 0.33%, a dividend yield of 2.35%, and its top holdings include iShares Core MSCI International Developed Markets ETF and Vanguard FTSE Developed Markets ETF.

feed.title: NextEra Energy (NEE) Q2 2026 earnings summary
feed.summary: NextEra Energy reported strong Q2 2026 results with adjusted EPS growing 9.5% year-over-year to $1.15, and adjusted net earnings reaching $2,407 million. The company is positioned for long-term growth through strategic investments in renewables, storage, and transmission, and anticipates a merger with Dominion Energy to enhance shareholder value. NextEra Energy also provided an optimistic outlook, targeting over 8% adjusted EPS CAGR through 2032 and 10% dividend per share growth for 2026.

feed.title: Lamb Weston (LW) Q4 2026 earnings summary
feed.summary: Lamb Weston announced its Q4 and fiscal year 2026 earnings, with net sales for the fiscal year rising 2% to $6,612 million, driven by strong North America performance. Adjusted EBITDA for the fiscal year reached $1,147 million, exceeding guidance, while net income was $290 million. The company provided fiscal 2027 guidance, projecting flat to 1% net sales growth, adjusted EBITDA of $1.1–$1.2 billion, and adjusted diluted EPS of $2.95–$3.25.

feed.title: Merck stock steadies as Keytruda drives Q1 2026 revenue growth
feed.summary: Merck & Co.'s stock is primarily driven by its oncology blockbuster Keytruda, which continues to propel revenue growth in Q1 2026. The company is investing billions in R&D to expand its pipeline beyond Keytruda, while also maintaining a significant cash return program. Keytruda remains a top-selling cancer medicine globally, and its performance, alongside pipeline developments and capital allocation, influences Merck's market valuation.

feed.title: Robin Energy prices $3 million public offering at $4 per share
feed.summary: Robin Energy Ltd. (NASDAQ:RBNE) announced the pricing of an underwritten public offering of 750,000 common shares at $4.00 per share, aiming to raise approximately $3.0 million in gross proceeds. The company, an international ship-owning entity providing energy transportation, intends to use the net proceeds for working capital and general corporate purposes. Maxim Group LLC is serving as the sole book-running manager for the offering, which is expected to close around July 27, 2026.

feed.title: Equity Residential suspends annual earnings outlook after merger agreement, quarterly FFO beats estimates
feed.summary: Equity Residential has suspended its full-year earnings outlook following its planned merger with AvalonBay Communities. The company reported adjusted FFO of USD 1.02 per share, exceeding analysts' estimates, and raised its full-year same-store net operating income growth guidance due to high occupancy and strong lease renewals. Despite higher interest rates, Equity Residential maintained positive operating performance in its key markets.

feed.title: Goldman Sachs Adjusts Ameriprise Financial PT to $562 From $550, Maintains Neutral Rating
feed.summary: Goldman Sachs has updated its price target for Ameriprise Financial (AMP) to $562, an increase from the previous $550, while retaining a Neutral rating on the stock. This adjustment comes amidst other analyst revisions and recent earnings reports for Ameriprise Financial, which showed improved performance. The company specializes in wealth management, asset management, and insurance services.

feed.title: GE HealthCare CFO Jay Saccaro to Step Down
feed.summary: GE HealthCare announced that Executive VP and CFO James “Jay” Saccaro will step down on August 14th to pursue an opportunity outside the medical technology industry. George Newcomb, the company's controller and chief accounting officer, has been named interim CFO while a permanent successor is sought. The company also released preliminary second-quarter 2026 results, expecting revenue and organic revenue growth, and reaffirmed its full-year outlook.

feed.title: Red River Bancshares (RRBI) investor Thibeaux reports 200,000-share, 3% ownership via trusts
feed.summary: Simeon A. Thibeaux has filed an updated ownership report for Red River Bancshares Inc. (RRBI), disclosing a beneficial ownership of 200,000 shares, which represents 3.0% of the common stock. These shares are held through two trusts, the John Charles Simpson Jr. Trust and the Angela Katherine Simpson Trust, for both of which Thibeaux serves as the sole trustee, giving him sole voting and dispositive power. The filing was made as a Schedule 13G/A, indicating passive investment under 5% ownership.

feed.title: Pest controller Rentokil challenged by slowing US growth - says analyst
feed.summary: Deutsche Bank analysts predict that Rentokil Initial PLC will face challenges in North America due to slowing industry growth in pest control. They expect North American Pest Control organic growth to decline to 3% in Q2 from 4.1% in Q1, influenced by weaker online demand for one-off pest treatments. Despite this, Deutsche Bank forecasts first-half revenue of $3.53 billion and EBIT of $562 million, maintaining a "Hold" rating with a 465p price target.

feed.title: Wells Fargo Adjusts Price Target on O'Reilly Automotive to $100 From $110, Maintains Overweight Rating
feed.summary: Wells Fargo has lowered its price target for O'Reilly Automotive (ORLY) from $110 to $100, while maintaining an "Overweight" rating on the stock. This adjustment reflects a revised outlook from the analyst firm. The article also notes other recent analyst actions and company news concerning O'Reilly Automotive.

feed.title: Newmont Resources delivers mixed second quarter earnings
feed.summary: Newmont Corporation reported mixed financial results for the second quarter, with revenue slightly missing analyst expectations despite achieving record free cash flow. The company's adjusted earnings per share exceeded consensus, and it reaffirmed its full-year gold production guidance of 5.3 million attributable ounces. Newmont also secured key regulatory approvals for its Red Chris Block Cave project, advancing it towards a final investment decision.

feed.title: These Analysts Increase Their Forecasts On Union Pacific Following Strong Q2 Results
feed.summary: Union Pacific (UNP) reported stronger-than-expected Q2 2026 results with adjusted diluted EPS of $3.41 and operating revenue of $6.864 billion, both surpassing estimates. Following these positive results, Wells Fargo and Benchmark analysts maintained their positive ratings and raised their price targets for the stock. Management also indicated that second-half demand is tracking above initial expectations.

feed.title: Bank of America hikes common stock dividend by 14% By Reuters
feed.summary: Bank of America has announced a 14% increase in its quarterly common stock dividend, raising it by 4 cents to 32 cents per share. This decision aligns with the bank's commitment to returning excess capital to shareholders, following strong second-quarter profits driven by global market activity and a surge in corporate dealmaking. Other banks have also increased dividends and initiated share buyback programs after recent Federal Reserve stress test results.

feed.title: SLB reports international growth as offshore activity offsets Middle East disruptions
feed.summary: SLB announced higher second-quarter revenue of $8.97 billion, driven by broad-based international growth, particularly in offshore activities. This growth successfully offset ongoing disruptions in the Middle East. The company saw strong performance in Production Systems and Digital revenue, with offshore developments in Latin America, Europe & Africa, and Asia contributing significantly to these gains.

feed.title: AI token routing cuts enterprise cloud costs with AMD
feed.summary: Advanced Micro Devices Inc. is advocating for AI token routing as a critical strategy to manage the escalating costs associated with enterprise AI deployments. According to AMD's John Hampton, companies are moving beyond initial AI experimentation and discovering that reactive infrastructure choices lead to exorbitant "token bills." AMD itself implemented this strategy internally, achieving a 43% reduction in token costs and a 2.9x increase in response speed by optimizing workload routing to more efficient hardware, such as their MI350P GPUs, instead of exclusively relying on expensive cloud-based frontier models.

feed.title: American Express lifts revenue forecast as card spending jumps 9%
feed.summary: American Express (AXP-N) exceeded second-quarter profit forecasts and increased its 2026 revenue projections, driven by a 9% rise in card spending from affluent customers. Despite the positive performance, the company's shares declined as investors focused on its unchanged full-year profit outlook. American Express plans to reinvest its overperformance into marketing to accelerate growth, especially targeting Gen Z and millennial customers.

feed.title: BlackRock Kicks Off $12.3 Billion Bond Sale for Meta Data Center
feed.summary: BlackRock has launched a $12.3 billion high-grade bond sale to finance a massive Meta Platforms data center project in El Paso, Texas. This move tests investor interest in AI infrastructure spending and comes amidst concerns about excessive investment in the sector. BlackRock subsidiaries and HPS Investment Partners own an 80% stake in the project, with Meta holding the remaining 20%.

feed.title: Lamb Weston Holdings Inc (LW) Exceeds Revenue Expectations with
feed.summary: Lamb Weston Holdings Inc (LW) reported strong fiscal fourth-quarter and full-year results for 2026, with net sales reaching $6,612 million and adjusted EBITDA at $1,147 million, both exceeding guidance, primarily due to solid North American performance. Despite a 19% decline in net income, the company demonstrated effective cost-saving and operational enhancements. GuruFocus rates LW as undervalued, with a GF Value of $70.32 against its current stock price of $49.18, suggesting potential for value investors.

feed.title: Invesco Ltd: Form 8.3 - Tate & Lyle PLC; Public dealing disclosure
feed.summary: Invesco Ltd. has disclosed its public dealing activities in Tate & Lyle PLC through a Form 8.3 filing, in accordance with Rule 8.3 of the Takeover Code. The disclosure details Invesco's interests and short positions in Tate & Lyle PLC's ordinary shares, showing ownership and/or control of 7,758,351 securities, representing 1.74%. It also specifies a purchase of 106,808 ordinary shares at a price of 5.57 GBP per unit on July 23, 2026.

feed.title: Oppenheimer raises NXP Semiconductors stock price target on cyclical uptrend
feed.summary: Oppenheimer has increased its price target for NXP Semiconductors (NASDAQ:NXPI) to $325 from $300, maintaining an Outperform rating due to an anticipated broad-based cyclical uptrend. The firm expects all segments to show year-over-year growth in Q2 results and projects data center revenues to exceed $500 million this year, up from $200 million in 2025. NXP also announced an interim dividend and a leadership change in investor relations, while other analysts like Cantor Fitzgerald and Barclays reiterated Overweight ratings.

feed.title: Norfolk Southern Raised Its Cost Outlook Right After a Strong Q2 Earnings Beat
feed.summary: Norfolk Southern (NSC) significantly beat Q2 2026 revenue and adjusted EPS estimates, driven by an energy-related demand surge. Despite the strong top-line performance, the company raised its 2026 operating expense outlook by roughly $600 million due to higher fuel costs, leading to a contraction in EBITDA and EBIT margins year-over-year. TIKR's model projects a muted multiyear return for NSC stock, valuing it at $380, indicating that cost pressures and merger uncertainty might temper appreciation despite current operational improvements.

feed.title: Intel Raises 2026 CapEx Outlook as AI Demand Lifts Forecast
feed.summary: Intel has increased its 2026 capital expenditure projection to over $20 billion, driven by strong AI infrastructure demand and better-than-expected second-quarter results. The semiconductor company also provided updates on its manufacturing roadmap, including plans for risk production of its 14A process by late 2027, with high-volume production slated for 2028. Investors are now focused on Intel's ability to tackle supply bottlenecks and convert AI demand into sustained revenue growth.

feed.title: Beyond PSPS: PG&E’s Wildfire Journey
feed.summary: This episode details how Pacific Gas & Electric (PG&E) is transforming its wildfire mitigation strategy, leveraging advanced technologies like AI and predictive analytics. T&D World's Jeff Postelwait interviews Andy Abranches, PG&E's vice president of wildfire mitigation, to discuss efforts to reduce Public Safety Power Shutoffs (PSPS), enhance grid monitoring, and build a more resilient electric grid. The conversation highlights the adoption of AI-enabled monitoring, expanded weather intelligence, undergrounding initiatives, and refined power line safety settings to improve safety and minimize customer disruption.

feed.title: OAKCHA EXPANDS INTO ULTA BEAUTY NATIONWIDE
feed.summary: OAKCHA, a fragrance house focused on accessible luxury-quality fragrances, has announced its launch at Ulta Beauty. Starting August 2, 2026, OAKCHA products will be available on Ulta.com and in over 100 Ulta Beauty stores, with plans to expand to more than 700 stores nationwide. This partnership marks OAKCHA's first exclusive launch with a national beauty retailer, aiming to bring its collection-building approach to

feed.title: CMS Energy stock trades steady as regulated utility earnings and dividend underpin valuation
feed.summary: CMS Energy stock, primarily operating as Consumers Energy in Michigan, maintains a steady valuation due to its regulated utility earnings and consistent dividend growth. The company's financials are driven by approved rate increases, strategic capital investments in grid modernization and clean energy, and a commitment to cost control. Investors value CMS Energy for its predictable cash flows, income-oriented dividend policy, and long-term investment plans aligned with regulatory frameworks and clean energy transitions.

feed.title: TE Connectivity 3Q 2026: Revenue $5.16B, EPS $2.55— 10-Q Summary
feed.summary: TE Connectivity reported strong third-quarter 2026 results, with revenue of $5.16 billion and diluted EPS of $2.55, representing significant year-over-year growth. The growth was primarily driven by strong performance in the Industrial and Transportation segments, including increased demand for digital data networks. The company also announced the acquisition of Astrodyne TDI and provided positive Q4 guidance.

feed.title: Third Business Unit of Tier-1 Israeli-Based Global Defense Company Places DefendAir Net Pod Order with ParaZero
feed.summary: ParaZero Technologies Ltd. announced a new order for its DefendAir Net Pod system from a third business unit within a Tier-1 Israeli-based global defense company. This order includes DefendAir Net Pods and dedicated integration support for the customer's autonomous Counter-UAS system. This marks the third business unit within the company to adopt ParaZero's DefendAir technology, emphasizing the growing recognition for layered defenses against hostile drones.

feed.title: Real’s Monthly Agent Survey: Competitive Economics Remain the Top Driver of Brokerage Choice
feed.summary: The Real Brokerage Inc. (NASDAQ: REAX) released its June 2026 Agent Survey results, indicating that competitive economics, including commission splits, revenue share, and equity opportunities, are the primary drivers for agents choosing a brokerage. While overall agent optimism dipped slightly and transaction activity moderated, affordability remains the biggest challenge for buyers, cited by 46% of agents. The survey highlights that agents seek a comprehensive value proposition beyond just commissions, focusing on technology, culture, support, and long-term wealth-building paths.

feed.title: Merck signs licensing deals for new HIV pill (MRK:NYSE)
feed.summary: Merck has announced seven licensing agreements with generic drug manufacturers, including Viatris, to expand access to its investigational HIV pill, alimatravir. These agreements are aimed at making the drug available in low- and middle-income countries. Three of the companies involved are from sub-Saharan Africa, notably Aspen Pharmacare Holdings.

feed.title: Walmart says goodbye to traditional shopping carts as it remodels 650 stores nationwide
feed.summary: Walmart is implementing new rules for the use of fulfillment carts in its stores, primarily for online order preparation, by reducing the maximum load to six containers and specifying when employees should push versus pull carts. This change comes amidst a significant e-commerce boom for the company and is part of a larger initiative that includes remodeling over 650 Supercenters and Neighborhood Markets across the U.S. in 2026. The remodeling aims to modernize the shopping experience with new layouts, updated technology, and personalized checkout assistance.

feed.title: ParaZero's Non-Explosive Net System Ordered for Drone Defense
feed.summary: ParaZero Technologies (Nasdaq: PRZO) received a new order for its DefendAir Net Pod system from a third business unit of a Tier-1 Israeli-based global defense company. This order, which includes engineering support for integration into an autonomous Counter-UAS system, expands ParaZero's engagement within the organization. The DefendAir Net Pod provides a non-explosive, net-based interception layer against hostile drones, emphasizing controlled neutralization and reduced collateral risk for various defense platforms.

feed.title: Home Depot stock trades near yearly highs as housing and Pro demand support earnings
feed.summary: Home Depot stock is trading near yearly highs, supported by resilient US home improvement demand, stable revenue, solid margins, and strong demand from professional customers and ongoing housing activity. The company's growth is driven by its interconnected retail strategy, disciplined capital allocation, and a diversified product mix that leverages both DIY and Pro customer segments. Investments in supply chain efficiency and technology further reinforce its competitive position and long-term value creation.

feed.title: DuPont stock trades steady as specialty materials earnings and guidance frame valuation
feed.summary: DuPont's stock is supported by its specialty materials portfolio, with recent earnings for fiscal 2025 showing net sales of around $8.4 billion and adjusted EPS above $3. The company's focus on Electronics & Industrial, Water & Protection, and other specialty lines, along with cost efficiencies, contributes to steady operating margins. While some segments showed growth, others experienced declines, and future guidance indicates a cautious but stable outlook for net sales and EPS, supported by ongoing efficiency programs and capital allocation strategies.

feed.title: Dollar Tree stock steadies as margin focus follows latest quarterly results
feed.summary: Dollar Tree Inc. (DLTR) saw its stock steady after its latest quarterly results, with investors weighing revenue growth against margin pressures from restructuring and pricing changes. Despite a 4% year-on-year sales increase in Q1 2026, operating income and net income declined due to higher distribution, labor, and shrink expenses. The company is accelerating store closures for underperforming Family Dollar locations and refining its product mix to improve profitability and long-term earnings potential.

feed.title: North America Agriculture Gateway Market Size, Forecast – 2035
feed.summary: The North America agriculture gateway market was valued at USD 1.07 billion in 2025 and is projected to reach USD 2.03 billion by 2035, growing at a CAGR of 6.4

feed.title: Wells Fargo Adjusts Price Target on Thermo Fisher Scientific to $660 From $615, Maintains Overweight Rating
feed.summary: Wells Fargo has increased its price target for Thermo Fisher Scientific (TMO) to $660 from $615, while maintaining an "Overweight" rating on the stock. This adjustment reflects continued confidence from the bank in the scientific instrument and service company's performance. The article also lists several other analyst price target adjustments for Thermo Fisher Scientific on the same day.

feed.title: GE Aerospace Collaboration Completes First Hybrid-Electric Flight Above 30,000 Feet
feed.summary: GE Aerospace recently concluded the 2026 Farnborough International Airshow with significant commitments, including approximately 1,800 engine orders and a record-breaking MoU with IndiGo for over 1,000 LEAP-1A engines. The company also announced key advancements like the first hybrid-electric flight above 30,000 feet, GEnx-1B engine selections, and certifications for LEAP-1B durability upgrades. These achievements underscore GE Aerospace's focus on future flight technologies and current customer delivery.

feed.title: Church & Dwight stock trades near its yearly high as earnings and household brands support valuation
feed.summary: Church & Dwight (CHD) stock is trading near its yearly high, driven by consistent earnings growth and a strong portfolio of household brands like Arm & Hammer. The company reported mid-single-digit billion-dollar annual net sales for fiscal 2025, with adjusted earnings per share increasing by a mid-teens percentage. This performance, along with a moderate payout ratio and ongoing share repurchase program, underpins investor confidence despite a competitive market.

feed.title: Lamb Weston Holdings Announces Quarterly Dividend, Payable on September 4, 2026
feed.summary: Lamb Weston Holdings, Inc. has declared a quarterly dividend of $0.38 per share of common stock. The dividend is set to be paid on September 4, 2026, to shareholders who are on record as of August 7, 2026. This announcement provides

feed.title: Tesla Short Sellers Gain $9 Billion as Shares Tumble
feed.summary: Tesla Inc. saw its shares drop significantly after reporting weaker-than-expected second-quarter earnings, leading to substantial gains for short sellers. Short sellers pocketed approximately $4.3 billion in mark-to-market gains from the one-day selloff, bringing their year-to-date unrealized gains to around $9.08 billion for 2026. Despite the current struggles and high valuation, investors are looking to future updates on robotaxis, robotics, and AI to restore confidence in the company's growth prospects.

feed.title: Truist Financial Forecasts Strong Price Appreciation for Global Payments (NYSE:GPN) Stock
feed.summary: Truist Financial recently raised its price target for Global Payments (NYSE:GPN) from $76 to $79, while maintaining a hold rating on the stock. Despite this, the broader analyst sentiment remains cautious, with a consensus "Hold" rating and an average target price of $83.46, as other firms have trimmed their price objectives. Global Payments exceeded earnings expectations, reporting $2.96 per share and $2.86 billion in revenue, and provided FY2026 EPS guidance between $13.80 and $14.00.

feed.title: CSX (CSX) Stock Faces Questions As Q2 EPS Growth Lags Bullish Narratives
feed.summary: CSX (CSX) recently reported Q2 2026 revenue of US$3,935 million and EPS of US$0.54, with trailing 12-month revenue of US$14.5 billion and EPS of US$1.73. While net profit margins remain in the low 20s, current earnings growth of 3.9% trails the 8.9% forecast, creating a gap between recent performance and market expectations. The stock trades at a P/E of 30.4x, above its DCF fair value of US$46.57, suggesting that some of the anticipated growth may already be priced in, leading to mixed views for investors.

feed.title: Intuitive Surgical Stock Is On Sale, But Is The Growth Story Changing?
feed.summary: Intuitive Surgical (ISRG) stock has dropped 23% from its recent high due to a slowdown in U.S. robotic procedure growth, despite strong demand for its new da Vinci 5 systems and robust international growth. This presents a critical juncture for investors, prompting questions about whether the dip is a temporary blip for a dominant company or a sign of market maturation. While the company exhibits strong financial health and a history of recovering from pullbacks, its premium valuation is being challenged by the decelerated U.S. growth.

feed.title: Intel, Amkor on AI optimism; eBay slips
feed.summary: U.S. stock futures rose on Friday as investors balanced geopolitical tensions with strong corporate earnings and AI-related announcements. Intel and Amkor Technology surged due to optimistic AI forecasts and partnerships, while eBay slipped after Meta Platforms launched a competing seller app for Facebook Marketplace.

feed.title: NEST expands data capabilities through Northern Trust partnership
feed.summary: NEST is partnering with Northern Trust to enhance its data and technology capabilities as it approaches £100bn in assets under management. This strategic 15-year partnership allows NEST's small investment operations team to automate processes, focus on higher-value activities, and leverage Northern Trust's expertise instead of building these capabilities in-house. The collaboration also prepares NEST for future challenges, including developing a retirement income solution for its members.

feed.title: L3Harris Technologies Inc (NYSE:LHX) Announces Quarterly Dividend of $1.25
feed.summary: L3Harris Technologies Inc (NYSE:LHX) has declared a quarterly dividend of $1.25 per share, payable on September 18th to shareholders of record as of September 4th. This dividend represents an annualized payout of $5.00 per share and a yield of approximately 1.7%. The company boasts a strong dividend history, having increased its payout for 24 consecutive years, and its 41.1% payout

feed.title: ETN|Eaton Corporation PLC|Price:415.130|
feed.summary: This article provides an overview of Eaton Corporation PLC (ETN), an intelligent power management company, detailing its various segments including Electrical Americas, Electrical Global, Aerospace, Vehicle, and eMobility. It also lists key company information, executive leadership, and shareholder details, noting that 3930 institutions hold shares, accounting for 84.22% of total shares as of 2026Q1.

feed.title: Super Group (SGHC) Stock Looks Fairly Valued Despite A Strong Cash Flow Case
feed.summary: Super Group (SGHC) stock has seen a significant 4.4x return over the past three years. While a Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) analysis suggests the stock is undervalued by about 49.5% with an intrinsic value of $28.70 per share, its P/E ratio aligns closely with fair market value, trading at 30.0x against an industry average of 23.3x. This mixed valuation picture, along with a proposed $1.49 billion share buyback and recent insider selling, indicates that SGHC is neither a clear bargain nor obviously overvalued, reflecting different lenses such as cash flow durability and market sentiment.
